rules.name: Orphaned subjects
rules.description: Checks that all ARMCD values in DM exist in TA. Subjects with unmatched ARMCD cannot be mapped to a trial arm structure, so epoch-level information (treatment duration, recovery period) will be unavailable.
rules.fix_guidance: Verify whether TA is incomplete or DM.ARMCD contains a typo. Subjects will still be included in analysis using DM.ARM label.

rules.name: Empty arms
rules.description: Checks for arms defined in TA that have no subjects in DM. Common for TK satellite groups or unused arms.
rules.fix_guidance: Empty arms are common for TK satellite groups that were planned but not enrolled. No action needed unless subjects are expected.

rules.name: Ambiguous control status
rules.description: Checks for inconsistencies in control group identification: subjects with dose=0 not flagged as control, control subjects with non-zero dose, or no control group detected.
rules.fix_guidance: Assign the correct control group using the dropdown in the Study Design validation mode. Comparative statistics require a control group.

rules.name: Missing trial summary parameters
rules.description: Checks that TS contains required parameters: SPECIES, STRAIN, ROUTE, SSTDTC, SSTYP. Missing parameters reduce study metadata completeness.
rules.fix_guidance: Add missing TS parameters. Values may be inferred from other domains (DM.SPECIES, EX.EXROUTE) where available.

rules.name: Dose inconsistency within subject
rules.description: Checks for subjects with multiple distinct EXDOSE values in EX, suggesting dose escalation or modification. Maximum dose is used for group assignment.
rules.fix_guidance: Review whether max dose is the appropriate grouping strategy for this study. Per-timepoint dose is preserved in EX for time-resolved analysis.

rules.name: Orphaned sets
rules.description: Checks for trial sets (SETCD) in TX that have no matching subjects in DM. Similar to empty arms.
rules.fix_guidance: Similar to empty arms (SD-002). Common for planned-but-unused TK or satellite groups.

rules.name: ARM/ARMCD mismatch across domains
rules.description: Checks that the same ARMCD maps to the same ARM label in both DM and TA. Mismatches indicate data integrity issues.
rules.fix_guidance: Investigate the ARMCD-to-ARM mapping in both DM and TA. The DM value is used for subject assignment.

rules.name: Categorical data in numeric result
rules.description: Detects LBTESTCD tests where LBSTRESN contains only a few distinct integer values (≤6), suggesting ordinal/categorical data stored as continuous numeric. FDA reviewers flag this as it affects statistical method choice.
rules.fix_guidance: Move categorical results to LBSTRESC. If ordinal scoring is intentional, document in analysis plan.

rules.name: Timing variable alignment
rules.description: Checks for NOMDY (nominal study day) presence alongside VISITDY. When both exist, validates alignment within tolerance. Missing NOMDY reduces the ability to compare planned vs actual timing.
rules.fix_guidance: Add NOMDY to findings domains if planned visit schedule is available. Align NOMDY with protocol-defined visit days.

rules.name: Below-LLOQ without imputation method
rules.description: Checks PC domain for below-LLOQ results (BQL/PCSTRESN null) without a corresponding SUPPPC record documenting the imputation method (QNAM=CALCN).
rules.fix_guidance: Add SUPPPC records with QNAM='CALCN' and QVAL describing the imputation method (e.g., 'BQL=0', 'BQL=LLOQ/2').
rules.cdisc_reference: FDA Bioanalytical Method Guidance, SENDIG 3.1 Section 6.3

rules.name: Undefined controlled terminology codes
rules.description: Checks key coded fields (DSDECOD, EGTESTCD) against CDISC SEND controlled terminology. Undefined codes reduce interoperability.
rules.fix_guidance: Map values to standard CDISC controlled terminology terms where possible.

rules.name: Early-death data in terminal statistics
rules.description: Identifies subjects with DSDECOD='MORIBUND SACRIFICE' or 'FOUND DEAD' that died well before the scheduled terminal sacrifice. Their inclusion in terminal group statistics can bias results.
rules.fix_guidance: Exclude early-death subjects from terminal sacrifice group statistics. Use the Early Death Exclusion toggle in analysis views.

rules.name: Cross-domain EPOCH linking
rules.description: Verifies SE domain element codes (ETCD) map to TA for each subject's arm, and that all DM subjects have SE records. Broken epoch chains prevent temporal analysis.
rules.fix_guidance: Ensure SE contains element records for all subjects and that ETCD values match TA for the subject's ARMCD.

rules.name: QTc correction documentation
rules.description: Checks EG domain for QTc correction method documentation. Flags when EGMETHOD is empty or when only one correction formula is present for non-rodent species.
rules.fix_guidance: Document QTc correction formula in EGMETHOD. For non-rodent species, include both Bazett and Fridericia corrections.
rules.cdisc_reference: ICH S7B, FDA SEND Review Guide

fix_tiers.name: Accept as-is
fix_tiers.description: Informational finding, no correction needed. The data is compliant or the issue is expected.

fix_tiers.name: Simple correction
fix_tiers.description: Straightforward value fix — typo, case mismatch, formatting error. Single-field update.

fix_tiers.name: Script fix
fix_tiers.description: Requires automated script to resolve. Multi-record or multi-field corrections.

evidence_types.name: Value correction
evidence_types.description: Simple A → B replacement for a single value.

evidence_types.name: Multi-candidate
evidence_types.description: Multiple possible correct values; reviewer selects one.

evidence_types.name: Code mapping
evidence_types.description: Controlled terminology lookup — maps free-text to standard code.

evidence_types.name: Range check
evidence_types.description: Value outside expected range or format.

evidence_types.name: Missing value
evidence_types.description: Required field not populated in the dataset.

evidence_types.name: Metadata
evidence_types.description: Structural or metadata issue in the dataset.

evidence_types.name: Cross-domain
evidence_types.description: Consistency check across multiple SEND domains.
